title: Can You Guess These 9 Brad Pitt Movies From JUST 3 Emoji? (We Bet You Get At Least 7)
dek: From chipped teeth to a severed head in a box to an actual torn Achilles tendon, Brad Pitt has left more chaos behind the scenes than any three emoji can really capture — but we're gonna try anyway.

items.reveal_text: Pitt actually asked his dentist to remove the cap on his chipped tooth so Tyler Durden could look like he'd genuinely been in a few fights.
New Line Cinema wanted to swap the infamous ending for something happier, but Pitt refused to make the film unless the head-in-the-box finale stayed exactly as written.
Pitt made Rusty Ryan eat in nearly every scene on purpose, figuring a guy on the run for a heist would never have time to sit down for an actual meal.
Pitt's famously bad Italian accent in the film was intentional — but here's the twist: off-screen, he actually speaks fairly fluent German, which he studied while researching his own ancestry.
In a plot twist Homer himself couldn't have written better, Pitt actually tore his real Achilles tendon while filming a fight scene, shutting down production for weeks.

The original third act was so incoherent that Paramount and Pitt brought in Damon Lindelof to scrap it entirely, leading to roughly 40 minutes of reshoots and a budget that ballooned past $200 million.

items.reveal_title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood (2019)
items.reveal_text: This role finally scored Pitt his first-ever acting Oscar (Best Supporting Actor), decades after his earlier nominations for 12 Monkeys, Benjamin Button, and Moneyball.
Pitt earned a Best Actor Oscar nomination for playing Oakland A's GM Billy Beane, on a film that scored six Academy Award nominations total including Best Picture.

This movie about two secretly assassin spouses is also where Pitt and Angelina Jolie met and fell for each other, a real-life plot twist that ended up dominating headlines way more than the film's box office.
Pitt reportedly did about 95% of his own stunts as Ladybug, and even wanted his character to accumulate visible bruises and lumps throughout filming until the crew talked him out of it.
